operating_state: Consolidated revenues grew 12% to $80.9 billion driven by 910,000 additional people served through Medicare Advantage and Medicaid programs, with growth concentrated in higher-acuity populations and Dual Special Needs Plans. UnitedHealthcare served 7.0 million Medicare Advantage members and 8.0 million Medicaid members while Optum Health expanded through organic growth in value-based care arrangements and business combinations.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio improved 140 basis points to 81.6% due to COVID-19 effects partially offset by business mix changes and decreased prior year favorable development. Operating cost ratio decreased 40 basis points to 14.4% from productivity gains offset by business mix and strategic investments. Consolidated operating margin expanded 130 basis points to 9.2% with UnitedHealthcare margins improving 140 basis points to 6.1%.
material_events: The company repurchased 12 million shares for $6.0 billion during the nine-month period and increased quarterly dividend to an annual rate of $6.60 from $5.80 per share in June 2022. UnitedHealth completed the acquisition of Change Healthcare on October 3, 2022 following the quarter end.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 continues creating uncertainties in care patterns with overall utilization near normal baseline levels but variability across care categories, while future care patterns may temporarily rise due to missed regular care during the pandemic. New COVID-19 variants could introduce additional uncertainties to care delivery patterns and business operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued broad-based competition in commercial risk markets while government programs face lower rate increases due to budget pressures. The company maintains pending acquisitions requiring approximately $14 billion in total anticipated capital while continuing to balance growth and profitability across all market dimensions and expanding value-based care arrangements.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing DOJ legal action relating to risk adjustment submission matters and potential changes in Medicare star ratings programs affecting revenue. Regulatory changes in healthcare laws and cybersecurity risks present ongoing operational challenges across the technology-intensive business model.
